articles.title: China Sought Access to Anthropic’s Newest A.I. The Answer Was No.
articles.summary: The latest artificial intelligence models from Anthropic and OpenAI are extending the United States’ lead over China and intensifying the rivalry between the countries.

articles.title: As Trump goes to China, what do Americans say about tariffs, Iran and world standing?
articles.summary: Most Americans see China as one of the United States' biggest rivals or adversaries, but largely as an economic threat, according to a new Chicago Council/NPR/Ipsos poll.

articles.title: Trump goes to China as Iran war smolders
articles.summary: President Trump says he has a great relationship with President Xi. His trip to China will provide a temperature check.

articles.title: The economic chilling effect of Trump's immigration crackdown
articles.summary: New research finds that ICE raids and deportation fears disrupted local economies, reduced work among undocumented immigrants, and may have hurt some U.S.-born workers too.

articles.title: Xi Is Poised to Press Trump on Arms Sales to Taiwan
articles.summary: Beijing has called Taiwan the “core of China’s core interests.” Xi Jinping is likely to focus on getting President Trump to slow approval of more weapons for the self-governing island.

articles.title: China Seeks A.I. Independence, Weakening Trump’s Leverage
articles.summary: Before this week’s U.S.-Chinese summit, Beijing reached a milestone in its quest for technological self-sufficiency.

articles.title: China Increasingly Views Trump’s America as an Empire in Decline
articles.summary: For decades, many Chinese viewed the United States with a mix of admiration, envy and resentment. President Trump’s volatile second term shattered that image.

articles.title: How Iran’s Internet Blackout Is Crippling Its Economy, and the Fight Over Fruit-Flavored Vapes
articles.summary: Plus, how everything became a subscription.

articles.title: Who Might Challenge Starmer in a Leadership Contest?
articles.summary: Health Secretary Wes Streeting, Mayor of Greater Manchester Andy Burnham and former deputy prime minister Angela Rayner are among possible contenders.

articles.title: U.S. ambassador to Israel says Israel sent Iron Dome batteries, personnel to UAE
articles.summary: Israel sent Iron Dome anti-missile batteries and personnel to operate them to the United Arab Emirates to defend the country during the Iran war, the U.S. ambassador to the country said Tuesday.

articles.title: How an ‘Impossible’ Idea Led to a Pancreatic Cancer Breakthrough
articles.summary: The new strategy also holds promise for lung and colon tumors. Here’s how scientists discovered it.

articles.title: 24 hours with 3 teenage birders: Welcome to the World Series of Birding
articles.summary: The three high-school birders, dubbed The Pete Dunnelins, have one day to count as many bird species across the state of New Jersey as physically possible. Here's what it takes.

articles.title:
The clipping economy: How short-form video 'clippers' are overrunning
the internet
articles.summary: Short-form clips of long interviews and shows are taking over the internet. But behind the sea of social media clips are marketplaces offering freelance clippers money per view.

articles.title: Drive 5 hours or fly 20 minutes? Remote towns suffer from lack of year-round flights
articles.summary: A federal program that pays airlines to operate in small and rural communities could have its budget cut in half, leaving parts of the country with no flight options.

articles.title: She's trying to outrun pancreatic cancer. Breakthrough treatments give her hope
articles.summary: Pancreatic cancer is notoriously lethal. But new treatments mean that may be changing, and people with the disease now have more reason to hope than ever before.

articles.title: Trump rejects Iran's ceasefire proposal response. And, Congress to tackle ICE funding
articles.summary: Trump rejected Iran's response to a U.S. peace proposal, calling it "totally unacceptable." And, Congressional Republicans are trying to push for three years of funding for immigration enforcement.

articles.title: Trump heads to China amid ongoing war with Iran
articles.summary: President Trump's trip to China is going forward despite the war with Iran casting a shadow over the state visit.

articles.title: Israel to hold military tribunal for Palestinians accused in 2023 Hamas-led attacks
articles.summary: Israel is planning a military tribunal to prosecute several hundred Palestinians accused of perpetrating the Hamas-led attacks of Oct. 7, 2023.

articles.title: Military drills in the Philippines affect the country's most vulnerable communities
articles.summary: Military drills disrupted fishing livelihoods in the Philippines, sparking protests as locals are caught between a great power rivalry and rising economic pressures linked to the Iran war.

articles.title: Israeli lawmakers set up tribunal, allow for death penalty for October 2023 attackers
articles.summary: The measure passed 93-0 in the 120-seat Knesset, or parliament, reflecting widespread support for punishing those found responsible for what was the deadliest attack in Israel's history.

articles.title: EU diplomats agree to sanction Hamas leaders and Israeli settlers
articles.summary: The unanimous decision by the European Union, agreed to on Monday, was sparked by growing outrage over the devastation in Gaza during the Israel-Hamas war.

articles.title: Nations Brace for Long-Term Economic Woes as Trump Calls Iran Truce Plan ‘Garbage’
articles.summary: President Trump said the fragile cease-fire was on “life support,” and India’s leader urged residents to cut back on purchases and travel.

articles.title: Dominion still has pending lawsuits against election deniers such as Rudy Giuliani and Sidney Powell
